scenarios.name: First Day at the Corporate Office
scenarios.description: It is 1986 and you are reporting to a corporate office for your first week on the job. The look should communicate ambition, polish, and unmistakable 1980s power dressing.

scoredItems.commentary: This is a strong 1980s business choice. Power dressing became a defining business look of the 1980s, especially in offices influenced by corporate ambition and gendered ideas of authority.
This is a strong 1980s business choice. Bow blouses paired well with suits and skirts in the 1980s office, balancing femininity with the decade's sharp tailoring.
This is a strong 1980s business choice. The pencil skirt remained a standard companion to power blazers in 1980s businesswear.
This is a strong 1980s business choice. Pumps anchored both 1980s power dressing and formal eveningwear.

incorrectSlotFeedback.shoes: Choose practical polished shoes appropriate for office wear in the target decade.

scenarios.name: Weekend Trip to the Mall
scenarios.description: It is 1987 and you are meeting friends at the mall for a relaxed Saturday afternoon. The outfit should feel everyday, youthful, and clearly mid-1980s casual.

scoredItems.commentary: This is a strong 1980s casual choice. Stonewashed and acid-treated denim were common casual looks in the 1980s, especially for youth and weekend wear.
This is a strong 1980s casual choice. Band t-shirts were common markers of music identity in the 1980s across rock, metal, and punk scenes.
This is a strong 1980s casual choice. Acid-wash denim is one of the clearest everyday denim markers of the 1980s.
This is a strong 1980s casual choice. Basketball sneakers became major fashion items in the 1980s through sport, hip-hop, and youth culture.

scenarios.name: Starting Work at a Design Firm
scenarios.description: It is 1996 and you are starting a new office job at a design firm. The outfit should feel professional but softer and more minimal than 1980s businesswear.

scoredItems.commentary: This is a strong 1990s business choice. Compared with the 1980s, 1990s tailoring generally moved toward less aggressive structure and cleaner lines.
This is a strong 1990s business choice. Minimalist dressing in the 1990s favored sleek knit tops in dark neutrals, often paired with clean tailoring.
This is a strong 1990s business choice. 1990s workwear often softened from 1980s volume toward straighter, less padded silhouettes.
This is a strong 1990s business choice. Minimal loafers suited the decade's pared-back office and smart casual looks.
